etymology.interpretation: 玫 (méi) combines the semantic 𤣩, meaning "jade," with the phonetic component 文 (wén) to convey the sound. The character is used to denote a type of jade, leveraging 𤣩 to evoke the preciousness and craftsmanship associated with jade.

examples.chinese: 她戴了一串玫玉项链(她戴了一串玫玉項鏈)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dài le yī chuàn méi yù xiàngliàn.
examples.english: She wore a necklace with rose jade.

examples.chinese: 玫石被雕刻成精美的饰品(玫石被雕刻成精美的飾品)。
examples.pinyin: Méi shí bèi diāokè chéng jīngměi de shìpǐn.
examples.english: The rose jade was carved into exquisite ornaments.

examples.chinese: 这种玫石在市场上非常受欢迎(這種玫石在市場上非常受歡迎)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng méi shí zài shìchǎng shàng fēicháng shòu huānyíng.
examples.english: This type of rose jade is very popular in the market.
